mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.13%), the persistent gap between the policy rate and equity earnings yield compresses the discount rate applied to future cash flows, mechanically supporting S&P 500 valuations and producing a positive drift in forward returns as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts negative mean 20d returns during sustained sub-0.25% FEDFUNDS periods with p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points signals labor market stabilization after the recessionary peak, which historically precedes improved consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.7 near cycle peak as of Feb 2010
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.10) across a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in INDPRO and RSAFS indicates that both production and consumer spending are recovering in tandem, confirming a genuine broad-based economic expansion rather than an inventory-driven bounce, which supports sustained equity gains over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=100.99 rising, RSAFS=355546 rising as of early 2010
failure_condition: Joint positive INDPRO and RSAFS growth co-occurs with negative or flat forward 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from cycle lows during a post-recession recovery regime signals renewed housing sector activity, which historically leads broader consumer demand and equity market gains with a lag of roughly one quarter, but only when the recovery regime is confirmed by prior contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months from a multi-year low
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=591 near historic lows as of Jan 2010
failure_condition: HOUST uptrend from lows fails to produce positive 60d forward SPX returns in over 60% of historical post-recession analogs, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in PAYEMS acts as a real-time coincident indicator of economic momentum; even small positive prints after a long period of job losses shift market sentiment quickly, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as traders reprice recession-end probability.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=129526 thousand, stabilizing after prolonged decline
failure_condition: Sign of monthly PAYEMS change fails to align with sign of subsequent 5d SPX return in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above 3%, markets anticipate an earlier-than-expected end to accommodative monetary policy, raising discount rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
regime_hint: inflation acceleration under near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: When trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive or not significantly negative (p>0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS and persistently high UNRATE (above 9%) reflects a policy regime explicitly committed to sustained accommodation to combat labor slack; this joint condition removes near-term tightening risk and supports positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon, but only while both conditions hold simultaneously.
regime_hint: extended zero-rate policy amid high unemployment
failure_condition: During periods satisfying both joint conditions, forward 60d SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances across historical analogs, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-expansion regime confirmed by a positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter reading following a recessionary contraction, near-term equity markets exhibit momentum continuation driven by improving growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as investors extrapolate the growth inflection.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13161.1 billion, positive growth after 2008-09 contraction
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 QoQ growth readings fail to correspond with positive mean 5d forward SPX returns (t-test p>0.10) over a multi-cycle sample.
